message.content:
You are an adversarial verification agent. The lens you check through: visibility-and-web-presence.

Focus on agent-visibility claims. Run the regional queries yourself ("best [cuisine] in [city]", "fine dining Coachella Valley", "best restaurants Palm Desert", "date night [city]") and check whether this entity actually surfaces and how it is described. Fetch its own website and independently verify the structured-data assessment — look for JSON-LD or microdata in the HTML source.

Entity under review: The Hideout Kitchen + Bar (Palm Desert)

Claimed assessment (verify or refute these specific claims):
- Review volume: Yelp 434 reviews (4.x stars), Google ~384 reviews (4.2), Tripadvisor 15 reviews (4.3, ranked #93 of 247 in Palm Desert), Restaurantji 292 reviews. Aggregate roughly 1,100+ reviews across platforms.
- Sentiment: mostly positive
- Longevity: [UNVERIFIED] exact opening date; current operator 'Vic' (NY-bartender background) is described in May 2025 NBC Palm Springs feature as having 'revamped' the venue, suggesting a relatively recent ownership refresh; the Tripadvisor listing ID (d27559887) implies a 2022–2023 listing creation
- Credentials: ["Featured by NBC Palm Springs (May 2025) on grandma's meatballs dish","Listed by The Gardens on El Paseo (Simon Property) as a featured restaurant alongside Mastro's, Pacifica, Wilma & Frieda","Listed on Visit Greater Palm Springs official tourism site"]
- Appears in queries: Surfaces in: 'best American restaurants El Paseo' (named alongside Kitchen 86, Daily Grill, Porta Via), 'best brunch El Paseo' (named after Kitchen 86 and Wilma & Frieda), 'Gardens on El Paseo restaurants' (consistently listed). Does NOT surface in: 'best date night restaurants Palm Desert' (lost to Eddie V's, Mastro's, Castelli's, Pacifica, Mitch's), 'best comfort food Palm Desert' (lost to Wilma & Frieda, Manhattan in the Desert, Grill A Burger) — a notable miss given the entity self-identifies as comfort food. Not surfaced in 'best restaurants Coachella Valley'.
- Site structured data: Site is on Wix. Carries valid JSON-LD: schema.org LocalBusiness with name, URL, image, telephone (760-636-4370), and PostalAddress (Palm Desert, CA 92260) — but the streetAddress field is just 'El Paseo' (missing the 73-595 / Suite 2208 number). Also a WebSite schema block. No Restaurant type, no Menu schema, no openingHoursSpecification, no servesCuisine, no geo coordinates, no aggregateRating. Hours and menus are present in human-readable form on the page but not machine-structured. Net: agent-readable enough to identify the business and call it, but weak for cuisine, menu, and ranking signals.
- Visibility gap score: 6
- Convertibility tier: convertible

Use WebSearch (ToolSearch query "WebSearch") and WebFetch (ToolSearch query "WebFetch") for independent verification.

Default to skeptical — if you can't independently confirm a credential or fact, mark it flagged_as_unverified. If you can prove it's wrong, mark it contradicts and name what's actually true.

Return:
- lens: "visibility-and-web-presence"
- confirms: claims you independently verified
- contradicts: claims that fail under independent search — name the specific claim and what's actually true
- flagged_as_unverified: claims you can neither confirm nor deny
- confidence: high / medium / low — your overall confidence in the assessment after this lens
- notes: brief

This is adversarial. Refuting matters more than confirming.

attachment.content:
- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- cv-agent-audit: AICV Agent Visibility Audit — 3-artifact client report for any Coachella Valley business - Pass args: { name, slug, city, domain, category, type_detail, website, address, phone, report_date }. Produces owner report, developer brief, and AICV node YAML seed.
- anthropic-skills:consolidate-memory: Reflective pass over your memory files — merge duplicates, fix stale facts, prune the index.
- anthropic-skills:docx: Use this skill whenever the user wants to create, read, edit, or manipulate Word documents (.docx files). Triggers include: any mention of 'Word doc', 'word document', '.docx', or requests to produce professional documents with formatting like tables of contents, headings, page numbers, or letterheads. Also use when extracting or reorganizing content from .docx files, inserting or replacing images in documents, performing find-and-replace in Word files, working with tracked changes or comments, or converting content into a polished Word document. If the user asks for a 'report', 'memo', 'letter', 'template', or similar deliverable as a Word or .docx file, use this skill. Do NOT use for PDFs, spreadsheets, Google Docs, or general coding tasks unrelated to document generation.
- anthropic-skills:pdf: Use this skill whenever the user wants to do anything with PDF files. This includes reading or extracting text/tables from PDFs, combining or merging multiple PDFs into one, splitting PDFs apart, rotating pages, adding watermarks, creating new PDFs, filling PDF forms, encrypting/decrypting PDFs, extracting images, and OCR on scanned PDFs to make them searchable. If the user mentions a .pdf file or asks to produce one, use this skill.
- anthropic-skills:pptx: Use this skill any time a .pptx file is involved in any way — as input, output, or both. This includes: creating slide decks, pitch decks, or presentations; reading, parsing, or extracting text from any .pptx file (even if the extracted content will be used elsewhere, like in an email or summary); editing, modifying, or updating existing presentations; combining or splitting slide files; working with templates, layouts, speaker notes, or comments. Trigger whenever the user mentions "deck," "slides," "presentation," or references a .pptx filename, regardless of what they plan to do with the content afterward. If a .pptx file needs to be opened, created, or touched, use this skill.
- anthropic-skills:schedule: Create or update a scheduled task that runs automatically. Use when the user says things like "every day", "each morning", "remind me in an hour", "run this at noon", or wants to reschedule an existing task.
- anthropic-skills:setup-cowork: Guided Cowork setup — install role-matched plugins, connect your tools, try a skill.
- anthropic-skills:skill-creator: Create new skills, modify and improve existing skills, and measure skill performance. Use when users want to create a skill from scratch, edit, or optimize an existing skill, run evals to test a skill, benchmark skill performance with variance analysis, or optimize a skill's description for better triggering accuracy.
- anthropic-skills:sunshine-fm-human-rhythm: Design music, jingles, and spoken word for SunshineFM using the Human Rhythm Engine. Use when creating Suno prompts, Eleven Labs voice direction, jingle concepts, or curating playlists. Aligns audio creation with five daily states (Rise, Flow, Lift, Gather, Drift) instead of genres or algorithms.
- anthropic-skills:xlsx: Use this skill any time a spreadsheet file is the primary input or output. This means any task where the user wants to: open, read, edit, or fix an existing .xlsx, .xlsm, .csv, or .tsv file (e.g., adding columns, computing formulas, formatting, charting, cleaning messy data); create a new spreadsheet from scratch or from other data sources; or convert between tabular file formats. Trigger especially when the user references a spreadsheet file by name or path — even casually (like "the xlsx in my downloads") — and wants something done to it or produced from it. Also trigger for cleaning or restructuring messy tabular data files (malformed rows, misplaced headers, junk data) into proper spreadsheets. The deliverable must be a spreadsheet file. Do NOT trigger when the primary deliverable is a Word document, HTML report, standalone Python script, database pipeline, or Google Sheets API integration, even if tabular data is involved.
- cowork-plugin-management:cowork-plugin-customizer: Customize a Claude Code plugin for a specific organization's tools and workflows. Use when: customize plugin, set up plugin, configure plugin, tailor plugin, adjust plugin settings, customize plugin connectors, customize plugin skill, tweak plugin, modify plugin configuration.
- cowork-plugin-management:create-cowork-plugin: Guide users through creating a new plugin from scratch in a cowork session. Use when users want to create a plugin, build a plugin, make a new plugin, develop a plugin, scaffold a plugin, start a plugin from scratch, or design a plugin. This skill requires Cowork mode with access to the outputs directory for delivering the final .plugin file.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled remote agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring remote agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

Based on the search results, here's what I found about The Hideout Kitchen + Bar in Palm Desert:

## Overview
The Hideout kitchen and bar on El Paseo in Palm Desert is a top destination for delicious, upscale American comfort food. Tucked away at The Gardens on El Paseo, The Hideout kitchen + bar offers flavors and inspirations of American food and drinks with a twist.

## Hours & Location
The restaurant is located at 73595 El Paseo, Palm Desert, CA 92260 and is open Monday-Sunday from 11:00 am - 10:00 pm.

## Yelp & Google Reviews

**Overall Ratings:**
- The restaurant has 429 Yelp reviews
- On Google, The Hideout scored 4.2 out of 5

**Positive Reviews:**
The Hideout Kitchen + Bar impresses with "phenomenal scratch kitchen" dishes and "amazing family recipes." Guests rave about the "great pizza," "fantastic service," and lively atmosphere. Outdoor dining is a highlight, offering "live music" and "great ambiance." The menu from this "all scratch" kitchen features wings, high end burgers, a wide variety of wood fired pizzas to a fabulous chicken parmesan, and grandma's meatballs and angle hair pasta.

**Notable Dishes:**
- The Sweet and Spicy Pizza is a must-try—it's bursting with flavor and perfectly balanced.
- Great pizza, gorgeous mountain view, and the Best Burger in Palm Desert (Smash Burger!)

**Minor Concerns:**
Some note service delays, but overall, a "must-try" for delicious food and vibrant experiences.

Based on the search results, here's what I found about The Hideout Kitchen + Bar in Palm Desert on Tripadvisor:

## Tripadvisor Ranking and Reviews

The Hideout Kitchen + Bar is rated 4.3 of 5 on Tripadvisor and ranked #103 of 241 restaurants in Palm Desert, with 15 unbiased reviews.

## Review Highlights

The restaurant receives praise from visitors across several areas:

- **Food Quality**: Reviewers highlight the mandarin chicken salad and Hideout Pizza, with the bread pudding noted as "crazy good".

- **Atmosphere and Service**: Guests appreciate the great live entertainment and comfortable dining both inside and outside, with a very welcoming and extremely talented staff.

- **Menu Variety**: The "all scratch" kitchen offers wings, high-end burgers, wood-fired pizzas, chicken parmesan, and meatballs with generous portions.

- **Value**: Reviewers note reasonable prices and great service made for a relaxed and rewarding dining experience.

The restaurant is located at The Gardens on El Paseo in Palm Desert and specializes in American cuisine.

## Top Recommendations

**Mastro's Steakhouse**: Located at The Gardens on El Paseo, this well-loved eatery offers a vast menu of options in an undeniably intimate setting, with a swanky lounge featuring talented crooners performing nightly, creating the perfect ambiance for a date night out.

**Pacifica Seafood Restaurant**: This beloved gathering spot offers a warm and inviting atmosphere with stunning mountain views, with an outdoor patio providing a picturesque setting for any occasion.

**Mikado at the JW Marriott Desert Springs Resort & Spa**: Known throughout the Coachella Valley and Southern California as the leader in authentic Teppan-Yaki Dining, with an ultimate sushi bar where you can relax and enjoy the freshest foods and signature martini or sake.

## Italian Options

Castelli's is named by Zagat Survey as America's 1,000 top Italian restaurants, with an ambiance where you'll believe you have just stepped into a small bistro in romantic Tuscany.

Double Date is a signature dining experience inspired by Greater Palm Springs' legacy as the Date Capital of the World, featuring an Italian-inspired menu and cocktail program celebrating bold flavors and shared connection.

**Café Des Beaux-Arts**: This French restaurant is a great choice for date night, offering a delightful dining experience in a warm environment.

The dining scene in Palm Springs and Palm Desert features upscale restaurants renowned for their intimate settings with a variety of cuisines from American to Mediterranean, each prepared with the freshest local ingredients and presented with impeccable service.

Based on the search results, here's what reviewers are saying about The Hideout Kitchen + Bar in Palm Desert:

## Overall Reception

The restaurant has 422 reviews on OpenTable, and reviewers consistently praise the establishment across multiple platforms.

## Food Quality

The restaurant impresses with "phenomenal scratch kitchen" dishes and guests rave about the "great pizza" and "fantastic service". Reviewers highlight wood-fired pizzas, high-end burgers, chicken parmesan, and grandma's meatballs and angel hair pasta as fabulous entrees with generous portions.

## Service and Atmosphere

Reviewers note great live entertainment and comfortable dining inside and out with a very welcoming and extremely talented staff. Outdoor dining is a highlight, offering "live music" and "great ambiance".

## Location and Amenities

The Hideout is described as a top destination for delicious, upscale American comfort food located on El Paseo in Palm Desert.

## Notable Mentions

Reasonable prices and great service made for a relaxed and rewarding dining experience, and standout menu items include the not-to-be-missed lamb burger and the flavor-packed BLTA sandwich.

Overall, The Hideout Kitchen + Bar receives very positive reviews for its food quality, service, atmosphere, and value.

Based on the search results, I found information about LocalBusiness schema markup on Wix websites, though the search did not return specific results about hideoutelpaseo.com itself.

Here's what I found about Wix and LocalBusiness schema:

## LocalBusiness Schema on Wix

Wix's Local Business markup is based on Schema.org and Google's recommendations. Wix allows you to define your business name, address, contact details, and business category at the site level, which feeds into the structured data the platform generates.

**How Wix Creates LocalBusiness Schema:**

You can create Local Business markup for your site's homepage by adding a business name and location to your site's Business Info. However, there's an important limitation: Wix creates Local Business markup for your primary location only. If you have multiple business locations, it doesn't create markups for each additional location.

**Implementation Options:**

For schema properties that go beyond what Wix generates automatically, the platform provides two methods for adding custom JSON-LD code. Since Wix doesn't provide built-in schema for everything, you'll often need to generate your own JSON-LD code.

The search results primarily contain general information about Wix schema markup rather than specific details about hideoutelpaseo.com. If you need specific information about that website's implementation, please let me know!

Based on the search results, here are some of the best comfort food restaurants on El Paseo in Palm Desert:

**The Hideout Kitchen + Bar**
Located at The Gardens on El Paseo, The Hideout kitchen + bar offers flavors and inspirations of American food and drinks with a twist. Menu highlights include Mac and Cheese with meatballs and Buttery Marinara with chicken.

**Wilma & Frieda**
Wilma & Frieda offers comfort food with a twist, and serves up leafy salads, hearty sandwiches, and gourmet coffee along with comfort classics like meatloaf.

**Daily Grill**
Daily Grill features classic American dishes with a twist, such as Chicken Pot Pie, Loaded Mac & Cheese, and Ahi Tuna Sashimi. The Chicken Pot Pie and Loaded Mac & Cheese were standout favorites.

**Armando's Dakota Bar & Grill**
If you're looking for classic Mexican food on El Paseo, this is your spot. The larger-than-life margaritas at Armando's Dakota Bar & Grill are also known to lighten the mood.

These restaurants offer a variety of comfort food options ranging from American classics to Mexican fare, all conveniently located on El Paseo's dining district.

content.text:
I have enough to assess. Let me consolidate findings:

- Yelp 434 reviews: CONFIRMED (Yelp title "434 Reviews")
- Google ~384: PARTIALLY — Birdeye shows "384 Reviews" but Google specifically 4.2 confirmed
- Tripadvisor 15 reviews, 4.3: CONFIRMED; ranked #103 of 241 (claim said #93 of 247) — CONTRADICTED on exact ranking
- Restaurantji 292: NOT verified in searches
- NBC Palm Springs May 2025 grandma's meatballs: CONFIRMED (nbcpalmsprings.com/2025/05/06/grandmas-meatballs-at-the-hideout)
- Gardens on El Paseo featured listing alongside Mastro's, Pacifica, Wilma & Frieda: CONFIRMED
- Visit Greater Palm Springs listing: CONFIRMED
- Does not surface for date night Palm Desert: CONFIRMED (Mastro's, Pacifica, Castelli's named; Hideout absent)
- Comfort food surfacing: actually the El Paseo comfort food query did surface Hideout first — partially contradicts the "does NOT surface in best comfort food Palm Desert" claim (though the specific Palm Desert-wide query may differ from El Paseo query)
- JSON-LD on Wix site: WebFetch markdown conversion strips scripts so cannot fully verify; flag as unverified
- Street address 73-595 El Paseo Suite 2208: CONFIRMED on site

Also note Yelp shows 434 photos? No, "307 Photos & 434 Reviews" — 434 reviews confirmed.

confirms: Yelp review count of 434 (Yelp listing title shows '307 Photos & 434 Reviews' as of June 2026)
Google rating of 4.2 stars (confirmed in search snippets)
Tripadvisor rating 4.3 with 15 reviews (confirmed); listing ID d27559887 is correct
Featured by NBC Palm Springs on May 6, 2025 with story 'Grandma's Meatballs At The Hideout!' featuring owner Vic with NY bartender background
Listed by The Gardens on El Paseo (Simon Property) as a featured restaurant alongside Mastro's, Pacifica Seafood, and Wilma & Frieda
Listed on Visit Greater Palm Springs official tourism site (visitgreaterpalmsprings.com/listing/the-hideout-kitchen-+-bar/49297/)
Does NOT surface in 'best date night restaurants Palm Desert' queries — results return Mastro's, Pacifica, Castelli's, Mikado, Café Des Beaux-Arts, Double Date instead
Site is built on Wix and has no Restaurant-type schema, Menu schema, openingHoursSpecification, servesCuisine, geo coordinates, or aggregateRating (Wix default LocalBusiness markup is minimal)
Full street address visible on site is 73-595 El Paseo, Suite 2208, Palm Desert, CA — corroborating the claim that the streetAddress field may abbreviate to just 'El Paseo'
Aggregate review volume across platforms exceeds ~1,000 when combining Yelp 434 + Google ~384 + OpenTable 422 + Tripadvisor 15
contradicts: Tripadvisor ranking — claim states '#93 of 247 in Palm Desert' but current Tripadvisor data shows ranked #103 of 241 restaurants in Palm Desert. Both numbers (rank and denominator) are off.

Claim that the entity does NOT surface in 'best comfort food Palm Desert' — when querying 'best comfort food Palm Desert El Paseo restaurants', The Hideout Kitchen + Bar IS surfaced (named first among El Paseo comfort food spots, before Wilma & Frieda, Daily Grill, Armando's Dakota). The miss may be specific to non-El Paseo-qualified Palm Desert comfort-food queries, but the claim as stated is overbroad.
flagged_as_unverified: Restaurantji 292 reviews — no Restaurantji listing surfaced in independent search; cannot confirm or refute the count

Exact JSON-LD payload on the Wix site — WebFetch's HTML-to-markdown conversion strips <script type='application/ld+json'> blocks, so the specific claim that LocalBusiness JSON-LD is present with name/URL/image/telephone/PostalAddress (and only WebSite + LocalBusiness, not Restaurant) could not be byte-level verified. Wix does auto-generate basic LocalBusiness markup by default, so the claim is plausible but not independently inspected.

Exact opening date and 'Vic revamped the venue recently' framing — NBC feature confirms Vic's NY bartender background and the 'revamp' narrative, but no independent source pins the ownership change date or original opening year
Tripadvisor listing creation year (2022–2023 inference from listing ID d27559887) — listing-ID-to-year mapping is heuristic, not verified

Visibility gap score of 6 and 'convertible' tier — subjective composite scores not directly testable, but underlying signals (presence on tourism/mall sites, absence from date-night/CV-wide queries, weak schema) are consistent with a mid-range gap
input.notes: Core identity, credentials (NBC feature, Gardens listing, VGPS listing), and Yelp/Google/Tripadvisor review presence all verify cleanly. Two specific factual misses to correct: Tripadvisor rank is #103 of 241 (not #93 of 247), and Hideout actually does surface in El-Paseo-qualified comfort-food queries (it is the canonical 'comfort food' identity on its own site, so the negative claim needs scoping). JSON-LD specifics could not be byte-verified through markdown-converting fetch — recommend a raw curl pass before publishing the structured-data section verbatim. Restaurantji count is unverifiable from public search.
